title: Bug: provider_job_id never persisted after OpenAI batch creation
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

## Problem
In worker.py _run_batch_ocr(), after the OpenAI batch is created (line 686), the batch ID is only logged (lines 687-692) but never written back to ocr_job.provider_job_id. The ID lives only in the in-memory batch_status variable used by the polling loop.

If the worker dies after create_batch_job succeeds but before polling completes, the OpenAI batch exists remotely but the DB has no record of it (provider_job_id=NULL). The batch becomes orphaned on OpenAI's side with no way to recover or cancel it.

## Fix
After line 692 in worker.py, add:
```python
ocr_job.provider_job_id = batch_status.ocr_job_id
session.commit()
```

## Location
worker.py:686-692 in _run_batch_ocr()

## Related
- GH-129: runtime orphan recovery for stuck OCR jobs
- Crosslink #1: job 1 stuck in OCR_STARTED (this was the root cause)
